traits.quirk: has an enemy (Aramil, a elf duelist, level 3, out there somewhere)
has an enemy (Gardain, a dwarf disgraced soldier, level 3, out there somewhere)

q01.name: The Ash-Callers' Rebellion
q01.desc: Shamans who traded the clan's fire for their own. The true shaman pays iron for them silenced.
q01.epilogue: The circle's ashes are scattered on running water; the clan's fire burns in one pit again, where it belongs.

q02.name: Wyvern on the Crags
q02.desc: A shadow over the herds, and herders who point at the high crags and will not climb.
q02.epilogue: The herders climb again; the skull goes up on a spear above the trail, warning and boast at once.

q03.name: Rival Warband
q03.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.
q03.epilogue: The rival clan sends a peace-gift of salt and knives; the herd trails run open.

q04.name: The Dragon's Tithe
q04.desc: A whole valley pays it tribute and calls that peace. The crown calls it rebellion with wings.
q04.epilogue: The valley keeps its harvest for the first year in living memory; the tithe-barns are rebuilt as granaries.

q05.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q05.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.
q05.epilogue: Carts roll the pass by the next moon; drivers still spit at the cave mouth for luck.

q06.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q06.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q06.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q07.name: Webs in the Canopy
q07.desc: The high walkways are silked over and the wardens who went up have not come down.
q07.epilogue: The silk is cut away and the walkways rehung; the lost wardens are carried down for their rites.

giver.name: Aelar
giver.role: the master of the high walkways

q08.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q08.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q08.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q09.name: The Coven in the Mists
q09.desc: Singers who left the circle and took the old songs with them. The mists over the hollow are not weather.
q09.epilogue: The mists lift over the hollow; the censor seals the stolen songs back into the circle's keeping, and does not say thank you.

q10.name: The Grudge War
q10.desc: A rival clan pressed an old grudge with new axes. The ledger wants balancing.
q10.epilogue: The ledger line is struck through in both clans' books -- balanced, the keeper says, for a generation at least.

q11.name: Things in the Deep Roads
q11.desc: The under-way to the second hold is silked and skittering. The toll-guild pays by the cleared mile.
q11.epilogue: The under-way reopens by the cleared mile, tolls half-price for a season in the party's honor.

q12.name: The Breaker in the Dark
q12.desc: Something big moved into the worked seams and it does not share. The mine masters want their tunnels back.
q12.epilogue: The seams ring with picks again by week's end.

q13.name: The Breaker in the Dark
q13.desc: Something big moved into the worked seams and it does not share. The mine masters want their tunnels back.
q13.epilogue: The seams ring with picks again by week's end.

q14.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q14.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.
q14.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

q15.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q15.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.
q15.epilogue: The recovered blades go back to the armory; the villages post no watch tonight.

q16.name: The Restless Crypt
q16.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q16.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q17.name: The Hedge-Wizards' Toll
q17.desc: Renegade hedge-wizards tax the road in fire and frost. The witchfinder pays for the tollhouse dark.
q17.epilogue: Carters use the road again by week's end; the witchfinder burns the confiscated grimoires in the market square, twice, for the crowd.

q18.name: The Rune-Fire Below
q18.desc: Chanters feeding forbidden runes in a sealed gallery. The runesmiths want the fire out before the stone learns it.
q18.epilogue: The vault is re-sealed under three locks and a new rune; the warden files the party's names under 'safe hands'.

q19.name: The Flooded Hold
q19.desc: An old hold, lost and lately dry again. What was buried with it did not stay buried.
q19.epilogue: The heir walks the dry galleries once, dry-eyed, and seals the hold behind -- some doors are better shut.

q20.name: The Rune-Fire Below
q20.desc: Chanters feeding forbidden runes in a sealed gallery. The runesmiths want the fire out before the stone learns it.
q20.epilogue: The vault is re-sealed under three locks and a new rune; the warden files the party's names under 'safe hands'.

q21.name: The Flooded Hold
q21.desc: An old hold, lost and lately dry again. What was buried with it did not stay buried.
q21.epilogue: The heir walks the dry galleries once, dry-eyed, and seals the hold behind -- some doors are better shut.

q22.name: Wolves at the Folds
q22.desc: Sheep gone, then a shepherd. The commons want the pack dug out of the hills.
q22.epilogue: Lambing season passes without a beast lost; the elder salts the den's earth as the old rites ask.

q23.name: Rival Warband
q23.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.
q23.epilogue: The rival clan sends a peace-gift of salt and knives; the herd trails run open.

q24.name: The Rune-Fire Below
q24.desc: Chanters feeding forbidden runes in a sealed gallery. The runesmiths want the fire out before the stone learns it.
q24.epilogue: The vault is re-sealed under three locks and a new rune; the warden files the party's names under 'safe hands'.

q25.epilogue: Carters use the road again by week's end; the witchfinder burns the confiscated grimoires in the market square, twice, for the crowd.

q26.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q26.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q27.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q28.epilogue: The ledger line is struck through in both clans' books -- balanced, the keeper says, for a generation at least.

q29.name: The Flooded Hold
q29.desc: An old hold, lost and lately dry again. What was buried with it did not stay buried.
q29.epilogue: The heir walks the dry galleries once, dry-eyed, and seals the hold behind -- some doors are better shut.

q30.name: Beasts Maddened by the Blight
q30.desc: Boar and bear alike gone wrong-eyed, tearing through the outlying groves.
q30.epilogue: The groves go quiet again; the keeper plants a sapling for every beast that had to die.

q31.name: Rival Warband
q31.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.
q31.epilogue: The rival clan sends a peace-gift of salt and knives; the herd trails run open.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Kildrak, a dwarf outlaw, level 7, out there somewhere)

q42.name: The Breaker in the Dark
q42.desc: Something big moved into the worked seams and it does not share. The mine masters want their tunnels back.
q42.epilogue: The seams ring with picks again by week's end.

q43.name: The Sealed Dispatch
q43.desc: Court business under wax, and a rider who never arrived. Someone would rather it never does.
q43.epilogue: The seal is broken behind a shut door; whatever it says, couriers ride the same road twice as guarded within the week.

q44.name: The Master-Smith's Commission
q44.desc: A commissioned piece, finished at last, and a patron in another land who paid in advance and waits badly.
q44.epilogue: The wrapping comes off to a long silence, which from this patron is the highest praise on record.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Anneth, age 10)
